Edward Sorin, C.S.C., 1842 Supporting the University's mission This college will be one of the most powerful means for good in this country. - Fr. Edward Sorin, C.S.C., 1842 The Notre Dame Investment Office is responsible for investing the Notre Dame Endowment and other financial assets of the University. Thanks to the generosity of the University’s alumni and other benefactors, and prudent long‐term investment and spending policies, the Endowment provides a solid financial foundation in support of the University’s mission today and for future generations. Spending from the Endowment supports numerous areas of University life Scholarships and Fellowships 41 Academics: Program Support 24 Academics: Faculty Chairs 17 General Operations 13 Athletics 4 Other 1 For more information, please see the University’s Annual Report A deep connection to Notre Dame’s mission and a true partnering approach with investment managers globally are the hallmarks of the Notre Dame Investment Office team. What We’re Fighting For By earning superior risk-adjusted returns, we support the University’s teaching, research, and service mission, providing students and faculty the resources they need to achieve success in their chosen fields. Committed Team The loyalty and dedication of our people to Notre Dame’s mission leads us to steward the University’s assets as ‘family money’ and treat every interaction and relationship with great care. Long-Term Investors Notre Dame was founded in 1842 by the Congregation of Holy Cross and will thrive for generations to come. As investors we embrace a long-term approach. Visit the Investment Office Notre Dame is on U.S. Eastern Time and observes DST (same time year round as New York City) 1251 N.
